fields.summary: Represents a four-byte tag used to identify font features, variation axes, and other OpenType resources.
fields.remarks:
<format type="text/markdown"><![CDATA[
## Remarks

`SKFourByteTag` is an immutable value type that encodes four ASCII characters into a single 32-bit unsigned integer, matching the `FourByteTag` / `SkFourByteTag` concept in Skia and OpenType. It is used throughout SkiaSharp to identify variation axes (e.g., `wght`, `wdth`) and OpenType features.

You can construct an `SKFourByteTag` from four characters, from a `uint`, or by parsing a string. The implicit conversions to and from `uint` make it easy to pass tag values across API boundaries.

## Examples

```csharp
var wght = new SKFourByteTag('w', 'e', 'i', 'g');
var wdth = SKFourByteTag.Parse("wdth");
uint raw = wght;
Console.WriteLine(wght.ToString()); // "weig"
```
]]></format>

params.c4: The fourth character of the tag.

fields.summary: Creates an <see cref="T:SkiaSharp.SKFourByteTag" /> by parsing up to four characters from the specified string.

params.tag: The string to parse. If <see langword="null" /> or empty, the returned tag has a value of zero. Strings shorter than four characters are padded with spaces.

fields.returns: An <see cref="T:SkiaSharp.SKFourByteTag" /> constructed from the first four characters of <paramref name="tag" />.
A four-character string representing the tag.
fields.summary: Returns a string representation of this tag by converting each byte to its corresponding character.
